text.title: AI Leverage Estimation Model Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules grounding the leverage-rubric. Each rule binds a binary check and a sourced rationale. Rule ids are referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every leaf task in the estimate MUST be assigned to exactly one of six task-classes: glue, scaffold, tests, business-logic, regulated, novel. Multi-class leaves must be split.

rationale.source: Internal — faion contractor throughput log 2025-Q4 across 134 tasks

rule.rationale: Multipliers differ by class by up to 4x. A leaf left unclassified collapses to "1.0x" which under-quotes glue and over-quotes regulated work, defeating the rubric.

rule.statement: The multiplier per task MUST be a band (low, mid, high), not a single point. Buyer-facing total uses the mid-band; sensitivity view shows low and high totals.

rationale.source: Hubbard, How to Measure Anything (2014); PMI PERT three-point estimation

rule.rationale: Single-point multipliers hide variance. A 3.0x-glue task that runs at 1.5x on a bad day blows the budget without warning; the band gives the buyer the same risk envelope PERT does on raw hours.

rule.statement: Each multiplier band MUST cite ≥1 evidence row from the operator's throughput log (task-id, date, planned-hours, actual-hours). Estimates without evidence rows are rejected.

rationale.source: Hubbard, How to Measure Anything (2014); FERMI estimation discipline

rule.rationale: Vanity multipliers ("AI makes me 5x faster") are the modal failure mode. Requiring per-band evidence forces the operator to use their own measured throughput, not industry hype.

rule.statement: Post-engagement, actual hours per task MUST be logged against the estimate. Deviations >2x the mid-band trigger a retro line in the engagement's lessons-learned record.

rationale.source: Deming, Out of the Crisis (1986); PDCA cycle

rule.rationale: Without measured deviation, the rubric never calibrates. Once-a-quarter retro batches surface stable drift in the operator's throughput, and the multiplier bands shift accordingly.

rule.statement: Quarterly the operator MUST re-derive multiplier bands from the last 90 days of logged deviations. Bands are versioned; estimates carry the band-version they used.
rule.rationale: AI tooling shifts the per-class multiplier every quarter (new IDE features, model upgrades). Stale bands re-introduce the original error the rubric was built to fix.

rule.statement: If preconditions fail (no past throughput data, single-shot work <4h, regulated novel domain with no AI exposure), the operator MUST fall back to plain PERT and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating a multiplier.
rule.rationale: A made-up multiplier is worse than no multiplier — it gives the buyer false precision. Explicit skip preserves the methodology's epistemic discipline.
